rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated scabies — routine treatment and household eradication with a safety-net for secondary infection/crusted scabies; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Scratched areas becoming hot, red, swollen, oozing pus, with fever; or widespread thick crusts developing
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary bacterial infection (antibiotics) or crusted scabies (oral ivermectin/infection control).
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the scratched skin becomes hot, red, swollen or oozes pus, or you get a fever, or thick crusts spread over large areas — get reviewed promptly.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Shared household with an affected spouse
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise simultaneous treatment of all contacts and decontamination to prevent reinfestation.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Apply the permethrin cream from the neck down tonight and leave it 8-14 hours, treat everyone in the house at the same time, wash all bedding/clothes hot, and repeat the cream in 7 days. The itch will linger for weeks — use antihistamines, not more cream. Watch the scratches for infection.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP in 2 weeks; prompt review if secondary infection/crusting
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless secondary infection or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document scabies (secondary infection/crusted scabies screened negative), permethrin + repeat, household treatment, and post-scabetic-itch counselling.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No infection safety-net or contact treatment, allowing complications/reinfestation.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Secondary cellulitis develops in excoriated skin, or the household is repeatedly reinfested.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Single-treatment/contact-omitting plan or no post-scabetic-itch counselling.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot dermoscope/scrape remotely, but the triad is characteristic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat clinically with permethrin + contact treatment + repeat, and safety-net for secondary infection/crusted scabies.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Single-patient treatment, nightly permethrin, or missing secondary infection.
